topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crumpled manuscript lay open on the coffee table, the pages filled with my own mundane routines, my embarrassing childhood anecdotes, and the exact shade of blue I wore to that dreadful networking event. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck, staining my cheeks a deep crimson. I stared at the words, a strange, cold knot tightening in my stomach. "You're writing a book about me?" I finally managed to croak out, the sound thin and brittle. He looked up, his expression one of bewildered innocence, as if I’d asked him to make breakfast. The silence that followed was heavy, thick enough to choke on. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the hardwood floor, and walked toward the kitchen, my jaw clenched so tightly my teeth ached.

[story 2]

I found the laptop hidden beneath a stack of old magazines, the screen glowing with a title that made my breath catch: *The Unfolding of Elara*. My hands trembled as I flipped through the chapters. Every anecdote, every private insecurity, every whispered secret from our shared history was meticulously documented, polished, and presented as narrative. A wave of intense heat washed over me, starting in my chest and spreading outwards. I slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing in the quiet apartment. "What in God's name is this?" My voice was sharp, cutting through the air like broken glass. I paced the room, my steps quick and agitated, unable to look at him, the sight of his calm, unsuspecting face unbearable.

[story 3]

It was the sheer audacity of it that truly stung. I hadn't seen the document until I was tidying up his study, and the sight of my life, so intimately known, being transformed into some grand, fictional masterpiece sent a jolt of pure fury through me. I picked up the heavy binder, my knuckles white against the cover. "This is completely inappropriate," I stated, my tone dangerously level, though the tremor in my hands betrayed the turmoil churning inside. He finally turned around, a slight frown creasing his brow. "It’s just... a creative exercise," he murmured, sounding genuinely perplexed. I let the binder drop onto the desk with a resounding thud. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to force my breathing to even out, but the anger pulsed beneath my skin, demanding an immediate confrontation.
